questions.question: a) What is web technology?
questions.answer: Web technology refers to the tools and techniques used to create, design, and maintain websites on the internet.

questions.question: b) What is the purpose of a web browser in web technology?
questions.answer: The purpose of a web browser is to access, view, and navigate websites on the internet.

questions.question: c) Define the World Wide Web (WWW).
questions.answer: The World Wide Web is a collection of web pages and resources connected through the internet.

questions.question: d) What is the function of a web server?
questions.answer: The main function of a web server is to store websites and deliver web pages to users when requested.

questions.question: e) Name the essential tags required to structure an HTML document.
questions.answer: The essential tags that are required to structure an HTML document are <html>, <head>, <title>, and <body>.

questions.question: f) What does the <title> tag in HTML define?
questions.answer: The <title> tag defines the title of the web page shown on the browser tab.

questions.question: g) How is the <img> tag used in HTML?
questions.answer: The <img> tag is used to display images on a web page. It is implemented as follows: <img src="image.jpg" alt="Description of image">. . The 'src' attribute specifies the path to the image, and the 'alt' attribute provides alternative text for the image.

questions.question: h) What is the purpose of a wireframe in web design?
questions.answer: A wireframe is a simple blueprint or sketch of a website's layout. It shows where elements like buttons, text, and images will be placed, helping designers plan the structure before building the site.

questions.question: i) How does an internal CSS differ from an external CSS?
questions.answer: Internal CSS is written inside the HTML file, while external CSS is written in a separate file.

questions.question: j) What is the role of the <form> tag in HTML?
questions.answer: The <form> tag is used to collect user input and send data to a server. It can include various input elements like text fields, checkboxes, and submit buttons.

questions.question: k) Define the concept of UX in web design.
questions.answer: UX (User Experience) refers to how users feel and interact with a website or application. It focuses on making the experience positive, easy to navigate, and enjoyable for users.

questions.question: l) What does <ul> include?
questions.answer: The <ul> tag includes an unordered list with bullet points. Each item in the list is defined with the <li> tag.

questions.question: m) What is HTML tag?
questions.answer: An HTML tag is a keyword enclosed in angle brackets used to define elements in HTML. For example, <h1> is a tag used to define a heading.

questions.question: n) What is HTML element?
questions.answer: An HTML element consists of a start tag, content, and an end tag. For example, <h1>Hello</h1> is an HTML element.

questions.question: o) What is HTML attribute?
questions.answer: An HTML attribute provides additional information about an HTML element. For example, in <img src="image.jpg" alt="Description">, 'src' and 'alt' are attributes of the <img> tag.

questions.question: p) What is meant by text formatting in HTML document?
questions.answer: Text formatting in HTML is used to change the appearance of text like <b>bold</b> or <i>italic</i>.

questions.question: q) Why do you use <div> tag?
questions.answer: The <div> tag is used to group together parts of a web page. It helps organize content and is often used to apply styles or layouts to sections of the page.

questions.question: Describe the structure of an HTML document with an example.
questions.answer:
An HTML document has a basic structure that tells the browser how to display the page. It includes <html>, <head>, <title>, and <body> tags. The <head> contains page information, and the <body> contains visible content.

Example:
<html>
  <head>
    <title>My Page</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <h1>Hello World</h1>
  </body>
</html>

questions.question: What are the differences between inline, internal, and external CSS?
questions.answer: Inline CSS is written directly inside an HTML tag and affects only that element. Internal CSS is written inside the <style> tag within the <head> section and affects one page. External CSS is written in a separate .css file and can style multiple web pages.

questions.question: How does the anchor tag <a> work? Provide examples of internal, external and anchor links.
questions.answer:
The <a> tag is used to create hyperlinks. It uses the href attribute to specify the link destination.

External link:
<a href="https://www.google.com">Google</a>


Internal link:
<a href="about.html">About Us</a>


Anchor link:
<a href="#top">Go to Top</a>

questions.question: Discuss the different input controls used in HTML forms.
questions.answer:
HTML forms use different input controls to collect user data. Common input controls include:

- Text box (<input type="text">): For entering single-line text.
<input type="text" name="username" placeholder="Enter username">

- Password field (<input type="password">): For entering passwords, hides the input.
<input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Enter password">

- Radio button (<input type="radio">): For selecting one option from multiple choices.
<input type="radio" name="gender" value="male"> Male
<input type="radio" name="gender" value="female"> Female

- Checkbox (<input type="checkbox">): For selecting multiple options or yes/no.
<input type="checkbox" name="interests" value="sports"> Sports
<input type="checkbox" name="interests" value="music"> Music

- Submit button (<input type="submit">): For submitting the form.
<input type="submit" value="Submit">

- File upload (<input type="file">): For uploading files.
<input type="file" name="file">

These controls help users enter and submit information.

questions.question: How do different types of HTML lists (ordered, unordered) work? Write examples of each and explain their uses.
questions.answer:
HTML lists are used to display items in list form. An ordered list (<ol>) shows items with numbers, while an unordered list (<ul>) shows items with bullet points.

Ordered list example:
<ol>
  <li>Apple</li>
  <li>Banana</li>
</ol>


Unordered list example:
<ul>
  <li>Pen</li>
  <li>Pencil</li>
</ul>

Ordered lists are used when order matters, and unordered lists are used when order does not matter.

questions.question: Explain the purpose of the <table> tag in HTML and demonstrate how to create a table with headers, rows, and columns.
questions.answer:
The <table> tag is used to display data in rows and columns. It uses <tr> for rows, <th> for headers, and <td> for data cells.

Example:
<table>
  <tr>
    <th>Name</th>
    <th>Age</th>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td>Anish</td>
    <td>15</td>
  </tr>
</table>

questions.question:
Write HTML code to create a simple table with headers and two rows of data.
Name Age
John 25
Jane 30
questions.answer: This HTML code creates a table with headers and data rows.

questions.question:
Write HTML code to create an unordered list of three items.
  • Apple
  • Banana
  • Cherry
questions.answer: This HTML code creates an unordered list with three items.

questions.question:
Write HTML code to create an ordered list of three items.
  1. First item
  2. Second item
  3. Third item
questions.answer: This HTML code creates an ordered list with three items.

questions.question: Write HTML code to create a simple form with a text input and a submit button.
questions.answer: This HTML code creates a form with a text input and submit button.

questions.question:
Write HTML code to create a complex table with merged cells using colspan and rowspan.
Subject Marks
Theory Practical
Math 80 20
Science 75 25
questions.answer: This HTML code creates a table with merged cells using rowspan and colspan for headers.

questions.question: Write HTML code to create a complex form with various input types including text, email, select, radio buttons, checkboxes, and textarea.
questions.answer: This HTML code creates a comprehensive form with multiple input types for collecting user information.
